description: Post-review fixes: (1) Wire VAD voiceActive_ from DnnDenoiser to Impl for dual worker loop, (2) Remove dead Rxx computation from WpeBeamformer, (3) Make export script fail hard when layer discovery produces no core layers (unless --manual).
steps: In dnn_denoiser.cpp: Add a std::atomic<bool>* voiceActivePtr member to Impl that points to the outer DnnDenoiser::voiceActive_. Set it during construction (or via a setter). Replace 'const bool vadActive = true' in the dual worker path with 'voiceActivePtr_->load(std::memory_order_acquire)' to read the actual VAD state.

In wpe_beamformer.h: Remove updateRxx() method, rxx_ array, rxxInitialized_ flag, and the call to updateRxx in process(). Update the process() logic to remove the rxxInitialized_ branch (the delay-and-sum fallback can use a simple frameCount_ check instead).

In tools/export_gtcrn_dual_core.py: After discover_layers() returns, check if encoder and decoder are both None. If so and --manual is NOT set, print an error and sys.exit(1). Only proceed with create_adaptive_wrapper if at least encoder or decoder was found, or if --manual is set.
acceptance_criteria: dnn_denoiser.cpp no longer has 'const bool vadActive = true' in the dual worker path - it reads from voiceActive_ via pointer
wpe_beamformer.h has no rxx_ member, no updateRxx method, no rxxInitialized_ field
export_gtcrn_dual_core.py calls sys.exit(1) with a clear error when discovery fails and --manual is not set
All files pass syntax checks (g++ -fsyntax-only for C++, python ast.parse for Python)
verification: grep 'vadActive = true' dnn_denoiser.cpp returns no matches
findings: All three review issues fixed: (1) voiceActivePtr_ added to Impl, set in DnnDenoiser constructor, read in dual worker loop. (2) Rxx tracking fully removed from WpeBeamformer (rxx_[257][2][2], updateRxx, rxxInitialized_, kAlphaSignal all gone); process() now uses frameCount_ < 3 for initial delay-and-sum fallback. (3) Export script now calls sys.exit(1) with detailed diagnostics when both encoder and decoder are None and --manual is not set. All files pass syntax checks (g++ -std=c++17 -fsyntax-only for C++, ast.parse for Python).
